## Subscription Tier Design (if SaaS)

### Recommended Tier Structure

```
FREE TIER — $0/month
Purpose: Acquire users. Remove barriers to entry.
Limits: {{usage_limits}}
Features:
  ✓ {{feature_1}}
  ✓ {{feature_2}}
  ✗ {{premium_feature_1}} (upgrade prompt)
  ✗ {{premium_feature_2}} (upgrade prompt)

STARTER — ${{price}}/month ({{annual_discount}}% off annual)
Purpose: Convert free users who need more.
Features:
  Everything in Free, plus:
  ✓ {{feature_3}}
  ✓ {{feature_4}}
Limits: {{limits}}

PRO — ${{price}}/month ({{annual_discount}}% off annual)
Purpose: Power users and professionals.
Features:
  Everything in Starter, plus:
  ✓ {{feature_5}}
  ✓ {{feature_6}}
  ✓ Priority support
Limits: {{limits}}

TEAM — ${{price}}/month ({{annual_discount}}% off annual)
Purpose: Small businesses and agencies.
Features:
  Everything in Pro, plus:
  ✓ {{number}} seats
  ✓ Team admin dashboard
  ✓ Shared workspaces
  ✓ SSO (optional add-on)
Limits: {{limits}}

ENTERPRISE — Custom pricing
Purpose: Large organizations.
Features:
  Everything in Team, plus:
  ✓ Custom seat count
  ✓ Dedicated support
  ✓ SLA
  ✓ Custom integrations
  ✓ On-premise option (if applicable)
```

### Tier Design Principles
1. **Free tier must be genuinely useful** — users who can't see value won't upgrade
2. **Each tier upgrade should feel obviously worth it** — clear value step-up
3. **Pro should be the sweet spot** — most revenue comes from Pro customers
4. **Team unlocks viral loops** — each seat is a potential champion who invites teammates

---

## Pricing Page Recommendations

### Must Include
- [ ] Monthly vs. annual toggle (with discount prominently shown)
- [ ] Feature comparison table
- [ ] "Most popular" badge on recommended tier
- [ ] FAQ section addressing common objections
- [ ] Money-back guarantee (30 days recommended for SaaS)
- [ ] Logos of existing customers (when available)
- [ ] One-line testimonial per tier

### Copy Principles
- Lead with outcome, not features ("Close more deals" not "CRM with automation")
- Address the #1 objection directly in the FAQ
- Price anchor: show highest tier first (or show it crossed out on comparison)

---

## Payment Infrastructure

### Recommended Stack
**Payment Processor**: Stripe
- Reason: Best developer experience, widest currency support, built-in fraud detection
- Integration approach: {{stripe_integration_approach}}

**Billing UI**: Stripe Customer Portal or {{alternative}}
- Self-service plan changes, invoice history, payment method updates
- Reduces support burden significantly

**Revenue Recognition**: {{approach}}

### Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Stripe account created and verified
- [ ] Products and prices created in Stripe dashboard
- [ ] Webhook endpoint configured
- [ ] Stripe Customer Portal enabled
- [ ] Test mode validation complete
- [ ] Production keys in environment variables (NOT in code)
- [ ] Failed payment retry logic configured
- [ ] Cancellation flow implemented (include save offer)
- [ ] Dunning sequence configured (email series for failed payments)

## Revenue Score Breakdown

**Total Revenue Score**: {{revenue_score}}/100

| Dimension | Score | Max | Reasoning |
|-----------|-------|-----|-----------|
| Market Size | {{score}} | 25 | {{reasoning}} |
| Differentiation | {{score}} | 25 | {{reasoning}} |
| Time to Revenue | {{score}} | 25 | {{reasoning}} |
| Revenue Ceiling | {{score}} | 25 | {{reasoning}} |

### Score Interpretation
- 80–100: Excellent revenue potential. Prioritize launch.
- 60–79: Good potential. Some challenges, but viable.
- 40–59: Moderate potential. Significant market or competitive challenges.
- 20–39: Limited potential. Consider pivoting the model.
- 0–19: Low viability. Monetization strategy needs fundamental rethinking.

---

## 30-Day Revenue Activation Plan

### Week 1: Foundation
- [ ] Set up Stripe account
- [ ] Create pricing page (even if rough)
- [ ] Implement free tier gating
- [ ] Set up basic conversion tracking

### Week 2: Acquisition
- [ ] Launch to Product Hunt / Hacker News / relevant communities
- [ ] Set up email capture for waitlist
- [ ] Contact 20 potential customers personally
- [ ] Offer founding member discount (30-50% off for life)

### Week 3: Conversion
- [ ] Enable paid tier
- [ ] Set up onboarding sequence (7-day email drip)
- [ ] Implement in-app upgrade prompts at usage limits
- [ ] Add social proof to pricing page

### Week 4: Optimization
- [ ] Analyze conversion funnel
- [ ] A/B test pricing page headline
- [ ] Interview first 5 paying customers
- [ ] Identify top-requested features (inform roadmap)

**Revenue target by end of month 1**: ${{month_1_target}}
